features.description: Implement the core counting logic of wordstats.sh plus the test runner. Create wordstats.sh at the repo root (bash + a single awk program, per mission/architecture.md): given a readable text file argument, print (1) the total word count, (2) the unique word count (case-insensitive), (3) up to three lines listing the most frequent words with their counts in descending frequency order (ties may break alphabetically). Also create tests/wordstats_test.sh: a plain bash runner that builds fixture files under mktemp -d, asserts the counts on the fixture text 'the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog the fox' (total 11, unique 8, top words the=3 then fox=2), asserts top-3 ordering, prints per-case PASS/FAIL lines, and exits 0 only when all cases pass. Run bash tests/wordstats_test.sh from the repo root and make it green before handing off. Argument-error handling and --help are feature f2; do not block on them (a minimal file argument path is enough for this feature).

Add CLI argument parsing, --help, and error handling to wordstats.sh (see mission/architecture.md contract). Required behavior: no argument -> usage error on stderr and non-zero exit; argument names a file that does not exist or is unreadable -> error naming that file on stderr and non-zero exit; --help (and -h) -> usage text on stdout containing the word 'Usage' and the tool name 'wordstats', exit 0. Extend tests/wordstats_test.sh with cases for all three behaviors (check both output and exit codes). Run bash tests/wordstats_test.sh from the repo root and make it green before handing off.

Write the repo README.md and handle edge cases. README.md at the repo root must document what wordstats is, usage (./wordstats.sh <file>, --help), example output, and how to run the tests (bash tests/wordstats_test.sh). Edge case: an empty input file must yield total word count 0, unique word count 0, no top-words lines, and exit 0 — fix wordstats.sh if it does not already behave this way, and add an empty-file case to tests/wordstats_test.sh. Run bash tests/wordstats_test.sh from the repo root and make the full suite green before handing off.

Scrutiny gate for milestone 1. Run the services.yaml commands verbatim (commands.test: bash tests/wordstats_test.sh) from the repo root and record exit codes. Spawn one mission-reviewer per completed milestone-1 implementation feature (m1-f1-core-counting, m1-f2-cli-args, m1-f3-readme-edge-cases); collect their review JSONs under mission/validation/1/scrutiny/reviews/. Triage sharedStateObservations. Write mission/validation/1/scrutiny/synthesis.json with status pass|fail and per-feature outcomes, then your handoff.

Flow validation for milestone 1: test every assertion TOY-01 through TOY-08 through the REAL CLI surface via Bash — actually invoke wordstats.sh (and tests/wordstats_test.sh for TOY-08) exactly as each assertion's When clause says, and check the Then clauses (outputs AND exit codes). Do not merely read the code. Save command transcripts/logs as evidence under mission/evidence/1/. Write mission/validation/1/user-testing/cli.json with a per-assertion result (pass|fail|blocked) and evidence paths for each TOY ID, then your handoff.
